name: Shakhrisabz
description: Shakhrisabz, ancient Kesh, is Amir Temur’s birthplace and the Timurids’ second capital, UNESCO-listed. Famous for Ak Saray, Dorut Tilovat and Dorus Saodat. In Qashqadaryo Region between the Oksuv and Tanhozdaryo rivers (~175,000 people, hot dry summers). Temur was born in 1336 near Khoja Ilgor; medieval names Kesh and Nautaka; historic centre inscribed 2000.

monuments.title: Ak Saray complex
monuments.info: Temur’s grand palace; the huge portal survives with fine tilework and the inscription: “If you doubt our power, look at our buildings.”

monuments.title: Dorut Tilovat complex
monuments.info: Religious ensemble: tomb of Shamsuddin Kulol, Gumbazi Sayyidon khanaka and the Blue Dome Mosque — among the holiest Timurid sites.

monuments.title: Kok Gumbaz Mosque
monuments.info: 1435–36 congregational mosque by Ulugh Beg opposite Kulol’s mausoleum; huge blue-tiled dome visible from afar.

monuments.title: Dorus Saodat complex
monuments.info: 14th c. “Abode of Power and Fortune” near Ak Saray: tombs of Jahangir, Umar Shaykh and Temur’s crypt; madrasa and mosque.

monuments.title: Chubin Madrasa
monuments.info: 16th c. in Chubin mahalla; UNESCO-listed area; Shakhrisabz history museum on site.

monuments.title: Malik Ashtar Mosque
monuments.info: 18th–19th c. neighbourhood mosque with a large winter khanaka and dome in the old town.
